fault_hypothesis: Line 70 contains a spurious post-processing step that removes the last element of `missing` when it equals `replica[-1]`, but this condition can be true when the element is legitimately missing (e.g., a trailing duplicate in primary added via `extend`), causing valid offsets to be incorrectly removed.

spec.expr: not (missing__1 and replica and missing__1[-1] == replica[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition on line 70 should never be true for valid inputs; when it fires it incorrectly removes a legitimately missing offset.

spec.expr: all(v not in replica for v in missing__1)

constraints.intent: Every offset in missing should not be present in replica (merge correctness invariant).

spec.expr: all(v not in replica for v in missing__1)

constraints.intent: At each loop iteration, no element added to missing should appear in replica.
After the spurious pop, missing should not have lost a legitimate offset — this branch should never execute for correct inputs.

spec.expr: all(v not in replica for v in __cbfl_result)

constraints.intent: All returned offsets must truly be absent from replica.
